title: Hannah Thomburn
content:
В В В В They lifted her out of a story
В В В В Too sordid and selfish by far,
В В В В They left me the innocent glory
В В В В Of love that was pure as a star;
В В В В They left me all guiltless of пїЅevilпїЅ
В В В В That would have brought years of distress
В В В В When the chance to be man, god or devil,
В В В В Was mine, on return from Success.

В В В В With a name and a courage uncommon
В В В В She had come in the soul striving days,
В В В В She had come as a child, girl and woman,
В В В В Come only to comfort and praise.
В В В В There was never a church that could marry,
В В В В For never a court could divorce,
В В В В In the season of Hannah and Harry
В В В В When the love of my life ran its course.

В В В В Her hair was red gold on head Grecian,
В В В В But fluffed from the parting away,
В В В В And her eyes were the warm grey Venetian
В В В В That comes with the dawn of the day.
В В В В No Fashion nor Fad could entrap her,
В В В В And a simple print work dress wore she,
В В В В But her long limbs were formed for the пїЅwrapperпїЅ
В В В В And her fair arms were meant to be free.

В В В В (Oh, I knew by the thrill of pure passion
В В В В At the touch of her elbow, or hand,
В В В В By the wifeпїЅs loveless eyes that would flash on
В В В В The feeling I could not command.
В В В В Oh, I knew when revulsion came rushing,
В В В В Oh, I knew by the brush strokes that hurt
В В В В At the sight of a sculptor friend brushing
В В В В The clay from the hem of her skirt.)

В В В В She was mine on return from succeeding
В В В В In a struggle that no one shall know;
В В В В She only knew my heart was bleeding,
В В В В She only knew what dealt the blow.
В В В В I had fought back the friends that were clutching,
В В В В I had forced back the heart-scalding tears
В В В В Just to lay my hot head to her touching
В В В В And to weep for Two Terrible Years.

В В В В Oh! the hand on my hair that was greying!
В В В В Oh! the kiss on my brow that was lined!
В В В В Oh! the peace when my reason was straying
В В В В And the rest and relief for my mind.
В В В В Till, no longer world shackled or frightened,
В В В В The voice of the past would be stilled,
В В В В Hearts quickened, cheeks flushed and eyes brightened,
В В В В And the love of our lives be fulfilled!

В В В В It was Antwerp, and Plymouth, thпїЅ Atlantic
В В В В And, so well had LoveпїЅs network been laid,
В В В В That I heard of her illness, grown frantic,
В В В В At Genoa, Naples, Port Said.
В В В В I was mad just to reach her and пїЅtell herпїЅ,
В В В В But a sandbank at Suez tripped me,
В В В В And we limped, with a crippled propeller,
В В В В Through all Hades adown the Red Sea.

В В В В Through the monsoon we rolled like a Jumbo
В В В В With a second blade shaken away,
В В В В There was never a dock in Colombo
В В В В So the captain drank hard to Bombay.
В В В В Then a пїЅpointпїЅ in the south like an anthill
В В В В Or seawastes, then hove into sight,
В В В В I called for no news at Fremantle
В В В В For I wanted to hope through the Bight.

В В В В ThereпїЅs a gentleman, reading, shall know it,
В В В В ThereпїЅs an earl who will now understand
В В В В Why I пїЅslightedпїЅ the son of their poet
В В В В (And a vice regal lord of the land),
В В В В Semaphore, and a burst through the wicket
В В В В On platform left guards in distress,
В В В В A run without luggage or ticket,
В В В В A cab, and the Melbourne Express.

В В В В пїЅTwas a brother-in-grief of mine told me
В В В В With harsh eyes unwontedly dim,
В В В В With a hand on my shoulder to hold me
В В В В And a grip on my own, to hold him.
В В В В A dry choke, and words cracked and hurried,
В В В В A stare, as of something afraid,
В В В В And he told me that Hannah was buried
В В В В On the day I reached Port Adelaide.

В В В В They could greet me, let Heaven or Hell come,
В В В В They could weep, for the grave by the sea
В В В В Oh! the mother and father could welcome
В В В В And the kinsfolk without fear of me.
В В В В For they watched her safe out of a story
В В В В Where she slaved and suffered alone,
В В В В They could weep to the tune of the hoary
В В В В Old lie пїЅIf we only had knownпїЅ.

В В В В But I have the letter that followed
В В В В That she wrote to England and me,
В В В В That crossed us perchance as we wallowed
В В В В That birthday of mine on the sea,
В В В В That she wrote on the eve of her going,
В В В В Hopeful and loving and brave,
В В В В To keep me there, prosperous, knowing,
В В В В No care save the far away grave.

В В В В They have lifted her out of a story
В В В В Too sordid and selfish by far,
В В В В And left me the innocent glory
В В В В Of love that was pure as a star:
В В В В That was human and strong though she hid it
В В В В To write before death in last lines,
В В В В And I kneel to the angels who did it
В В В В And I bow to the fate that refines.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Henry Lawson delivers a powerful performance in "Hannah Thomburn"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
